title: Feasibility Denial
desc: VRC claims training or employment is not reasonably feasible because your disabilities are too severe.

fields.name: hasDoctorLetter
fields.label: Do you have a letter from a physician supporting training?

fields.name: resolvedDoubt
fields.label: Did the VRC resolve reasonable doubts in your favor under 38 C.F.R. § 21.57?

fields.name: extendedEvalOffered
fields.label: Did the VRC offer or evaluate you for an Extended Evaluation plan?

fields.name: personalFeasibilityRemarks
fields.label: Explain why you believe the goal is feasible (e.g., successful courses, recent volunteering, or accommodations that work)
fields.placeholder: e.g. I successfully completed two accounting courses online with a 3.8 GPA.

errors: Failing to resolve reasonable doubt in favor of feasibility as mandated by 38 C.F.R. § 21.57.
Denying feasibility without evaluating or offering an Extended Evaluation (38 C.F.R. § 21.74) to fully test capabilities.

evidenceChecklist.text: Physician letter stating training/work is possible with accommodations
Records of recent volunteer work, courses completed, or active hobbies
Copy of the VRC vocational assessment detailing unfeasibility reasons

bestNextActionRules.criteria: VRC asserts unfeasibility without extended evaluation
bestNextActionRules.action: Formally request a 12-month Extended Evaluation under 38 C.F.R. § 21.74 to test capacity.

reviewLaneQuestions.question: Did the counselor offer an Extended Evaluation to test feasibility before closing?
Do you have a treating doctor who supports your ability to participate in this training?
